app.workflow_caption: Auth → Channels → Parse → Search → Analytics → Files
app.nav_guard_warning: Parsing is currently running. If you leave this page, **all progress will be lost** and you will need to start over.
auth.caption: Step 1 — verify your Telegram session before parsing
auth.help_step1_header: ### Step 1 — Get API ID & API Hash
auth.help_step1_body:
1. Go to [my.telegram.org/apps](https://my.telegram.org/apps)
2. Log in with your phone number
3. Click **API development tools**
4. Fill in any app title and short name (e.g. `tg-harvest`, `harvest`)
5. Copy **App api_id** and **App api_hash**
auth.help_step2_body: Create a `.env` file in the project root with your credentials:
auth.help_step3_body: Run the login command in terminal. You will receive a code in Telegram:
auth.help_private_body:
- Your account must be a **member** of the channel
- Use the **numeric channel ID** (e.g. `-1001234567890`) to parse private channels
- You can find channel IDs on the **Channels** page after logging in
- TG Harvest works at the MTProto level — it can extract data even from channels with copy restrictions
auth.no_credentials_info: Open the **How to get API credentials** section above for a step-by-step guide.
auth.session_not_authorized: Session file exists but is not authorized.
auth.no_session_steps:
1. Make sure `.env` file exists with your API credentials
2. Run the login command in terminal:
auth.connect_error_info: Check your internet connection and try again.
channels.caption: Step 2 — browse your accessible channels, groups, bots, and private chats
channels.tips_body:
- Click **Load channels** to see all channels and groups your account has access to
- **Private channels** have no username — use their **numeric ID** for parsing
- Copy the ID from the table below and paste it on the **Parse** page
- The 🔒 column shows channels that Telegram has flagged as restricted
- Increase **Max dialogs** in the sidebar if you don’t see all your channels
channels.error_auth: Not authorized. Go to **Auth Status** and log in first.
channels.empty_state_info: Click **Load channels** to see your accessible channels, groups, bots, and private chats.
channels.empty_go_parse_hint: Once loaded, copy a channel ID and go to the **Parse** page to start parsing.
parser.caption: Step 3 — extract messages from a Telegram channel, group, bot, or private chat
parser.tips_body:
**Channel formats:**
- `@channel_name` — public channel by username
- `-1001234567890` — private channel by numeric ID (find IDs on the **Channels** page)

**Options:**
- **Incremental** — only fetch messages newer than the last parse (saves time on repeated parses)
- **Date range** — limit parsing to a specific period (optional)
- **Message limit** — set `0` for no limit, or a specific number to cap results
- **Field selection** — choose which fields to include in the export

**Private & restricted channels:**
- Your account must be a member of the channel
- Channels with copy restrictions are fully supported — TG Harvest works at the MTProto level
parser.no_channels_warning: No channels loaded yet. Go to the **Channels** page first to load your channel list, then come back here to parse.
parser.channel_load_hint: Load channels on the **Channels** page first to see them here
parser.incremental_help: Only fetch new messages since last parse
parser.incremental_caption: Will fetch only messages newer than the last parse.
parser.fields_expander: Field selection (select which fields to export)
parser.empty_state_info: Enter a channel username (@channel) or numeric ID to start parsing.
parser.empty_state_hint: Go to the **Channels** page to browse available channels and copy their IDs.
parser.flood_wait_info: Telegram may throttle requests (~every 3000 messages). The parser will auto-pause for 30–60 sec and continue. Don't close the page.
parser.error_auth: Authentication error. Go to **Auth Status** page and verify your session.
parser.error_flood: Telegram flood wait: please wait {seconds} seconds before retrying.
parser.error_not_found: Channel not found: `{channel}`. Check the username or ID.
parser.error_network: Network error. Check your internet connection and try again.
parser.text_only_help: Show only message text in results and exports, without metadata
parser.parse_text_only_help: Parse only message text — skip media, reactions, forwards for faster parsing
search.caption: Step 4 — find messages across all parsed data
search.no_data_warning: No parsed data found. Go to **Parse** page first.
search.tips_body:
- Enter keywords to search across all parsed messages
- Use **Filters** to narrow results by media type, views, reactions, date range, or specific channel
- Leave the search query empty and click **Search** to browse all messages with applied filters
- Results are limited to the number set in the sidebar — use filters to narrow down
- Click **Download results as CSV** to export the search results
search.empty_state_info: Enter a search query to find messages across all parsed data.
search.no_results_warning: No messages found matching your criteria.
analytics.caption: Step 5 — visualize channel activity and engagement
analytics.no_data_warning: No parsed data found. Go to **Parse** page first.
analytics.tips_body:
- Select a parsed dataset from the dropdown to view its analytics
- **Avg views / reactions** — average per message; `0` means the channel doesn’t expose this data
- **Messages per Day** — shows posting frequency over time
- **Activity by Hour** — times are in UTC; helps identify the channel’s peak activity hours
- **Top by Views / Reactions** — most popular messages by engagement
- **Media Distribution** — breakdown of message types (text, photos, videos, documents, etc.)
- Parse a channel multiple times (with **Incremental** mode) to track changes over time
analytics.top_views_empty: No view data. Parse a channel that tracks views.
analytics.top_reactions_empty: No reaction data. Parse a channel with reactions enabled.
analytics.reactions_breakdown_empty: No reaction data. Parse a channel with reactions enabled.
analytics.compare_hint: Select at least 2 datasets to compare
files.caption: View, delete, and re-export parsed files
files.no_files_warning: No parsed files found. Go to **Parse** page first.
parser.download_media_help: Download photos, videos, and documents during parsing
parser.fetch_replies_help: Fetch full reply thread messages (extra API calls, may be slow)
parser.enrich_senders_help: Resolve sender IDs to usernames and display names
parser.split_parts_help: Split download into N files (2-10). Useful for large exports. Files are packed into a ZIP archive.
parser.split_info: To split output into multiple files, use the **Split into parts** option that appears in the results section after parsing.
parser.download_all_split_help: Split each channel's messages into N files inside the archive
